title: petasites frigidus var. genuinus
intro: Petasites frigidus var. genuinus, also known as Butterbur, is a perennial plant native to Europe and North America, characterized by its large, oval-shaped leaves and yellowish flowers. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The medicinal properties of Butterbur are said to provide various health benefits, including reducing migraine and tension headaches, relieving symptoms of allergies and asthma, and supporting digestive health. Butterbur is commonly prepared as a dried extract or tea, and can also be taken in capsule or tablet form as a dietary supplement.
